title: 3. History and Trend of Cosmetic Acupuncture
text:
Although the history of cosmetic acupuncture can date back to the ancient doctor 'Sun
Simiao' of A.D. 700 or a possibly much earlier, cosmetic acupuncture in western society
emerged around the early 1980s. At that time, acupuncture treatment was more focused on
the distal point to regulate qi energy. In the mid 2000s, facial acupuncture became popular in
Korea, Japan, and China, and much research has since been conducted on the topic.
Ironically, TCM doctors in the U.S. are a lot more 'conventional and traditional' in their
practice of TCM. I use the term 'conventional and traditional' to refer to orthodox TCM, not
conventional medicine. They focus more on using traditional ways of regulating qi and
diagnosis in cosmetic acupuncture.
On the other hand, in China and Korea, various new treatment methods have been
practiced in the clinical ﬁeld. Other treatments besides acupuncture such as cupping, gua-
sha, ear acupuncture, and moxibustion are popular.
Cosmetic acupuncture got popular again in the 1990s with economic growth in China.
Since then, various techniques have been developed.  Japanese cosmetic acupuncture is more
1
focused on local tissue needling around wrinkles. They use a great quantity of needles and e-
stimulation.
Myofascial  releasing  and  balancing  are  popular  approaches  in  Korean  cosmetic
acupuncture. They were originally used for treating facial nerve palsy, a TMJ disorder, or
facial asymmetry. There are several famous techniques with myofascial approaches like,
Jung-an Acupuncture and Miso facial rejuvenation acupuncture.  Pharmacopuncture (herbal
2
extract  injection)  and  thread  embedding  acupuncture  (transplanting  a  surgical  thread
3
beneath a wrinkle) are unique Korean acupuncture techniques.
Virginia Doran. An Introduction to Facial Revitalisation Acupuncture. The European Journal of Oriental Medicine,
1
2007;5(7).
MJ Im, et al. Study on the Local Wrinkle Cure Using Acupuncture Needle. Korean J. Oriental Physiology & Pathology,
2
2009;23(6):1261-1266.
YH Yun, et al. Narrative Review and Propose of Thread Embedding Acupuncture Procedure for Facial Wrinkles and
3
Facial Laxity. Korean Med Ophthalmol Otolaryngol Dermatol, 2015;28(1):119-133.
